name: Zarr (Chunked N-D Arrays)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Zarr specification is open

auth.notes: Local/cloud storage; cloud via fsspec (S3, GCS, Azure Blob)

performance.notes: Chunked storage enables parallel I/O; cloud-native design; concurrent reads/writes

agent_readiness.notes: Chunked, compressed, N-dimensional array storage. Cloud-native: stores arrays on S3, GCS, Azure Blob, local filesystem. Zarr v3 is the current spec. Compression codecs (LZ4, Blosc, Zstd, GZIP). Multiple data types. Concurrent reads via multiple threads/processes. Used with xarray and Dask for out-of-core scientific computing. Alternative to HDF5 for cloud storage (HDF5 doesn't support cloud-native access). Used in bioinformatics (FOSS CellxGene), geosciences, ML datasets.

gotchas: Zarr v2 vs v3 compatibility — migration needed for v3
Not a database — no query language or metadata search
Cloud I/O latency dominates for small chunk sizes
best_when: Large scientific datasets requiring parallel I/O
xarray/Dask workflows with cloud storage backend
ML datasets stored in chunked format for fast random access
avoid_when: When HDF5's broader toolchain is required
summary: Zarr is a cloud-native chunked array storage format. Stores N-dimensional arrays on S3, GCS, Azure Blob, or local filesystem with compression and parallel I/O. The cloud-native alternative to HDF5. Works seamlessly with xarray and Dask. Used in geosciences, bioinformatics, and ML for large scientific datasets needing fast random access from cloud storage.
